---
title: "Instagram"
platform_slug: "instagram"
sources:
  ugc:
    BR: data/global/instagram/ugc.yml
    EU: data/global/instagram/ugc.yml
    UK: data/global/instagram/ugc.yml
  ads:
    BR: data/regional/BR/meta/ads.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/meta/ads.yml
    UK: data/regional/UK/meta/ads.yml
---

Instagram’s UGC transparency is assessed through the [Meta Content Library](https://transparency.meta.com/researchtools/meta-content-library/) as a single global resource applied across Brazil, the EU, and the UK, while advertising transparency draws on the [Meta Ad Library](https://www.facebook.com/ads/library), with region-specific access regimes. The Meta Content Library provides access to data from Instagram and other Meta platforms under very strict conditions: although it offers an API, its terms prohibit structured data extraction for external analysis, effectively limiting access and analysis to a pre-approved Secure Research Environment and rendering it unavailable for independent research purposes. As a result, Instagram scores minimally on UGC transparency, since—unlike the API—the Meta Content Library User Interface allows approved researchers to visualize and extract structured data on user-generated content across Meta platforms, but only above certain popularity-based follower thresholds that restrict data availability. Advertising transparency varies by region: the Meta Ad Library in the EU and the UK systematically covers all ad types served over the past year, though the UK version differs from the EU in that its GUI enables structured data extraction for ads labeled as political and “social issue” ads, which are banned in the EU. In Brazil, the tool is largely limited to ads labeled as political and “social issue” ads, as other ads can only be viewed while actively running and cannot be systematically extracted. Across all regions, the library provides free access, unique advertiser identifiers, and ad delivery periods, but lacks engagement metrics, granular spending data, complete targeting criteria, and specific rate-limit documentation, with the availability of such information varying by ad type.

## Transparency Assessment


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path
project_root = Path.cwd()
while not (project_root / 'utils').exists() and project_root != project_root.parent:
    project_root = project_root.parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))
from utils.responses_aggregator import generate_platform_question_sections
```

```{python}
#| label: platform-summary
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from utils.platform_summary import generate_platform_summary
generate_platform_summary("instagram")
```




## User-Generated Content

```{python}
#| label: ugc-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="instagram",
    question_type="ugc",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## Advertising

```{python}
#| label: ads-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="instagram",
    question_type="ads",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```
